---
title: "Quality Assurance Phenotypic Report"
author: "Contact:<a href = 'https://github.com/Breeding-Analytics/bioflow' target = '_blank'>Breeding Analytics Team, OneCGIAR</a> breedinganalytics@cgiar.org"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"  
output: html_document
params:
  toDownload: TRUE
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# knitr R markdown chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(dependson = knitr::all_labels(),
                      echo = FALSE,
                      cache = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      comment = NA,
                      out.width = "100%",
                      error = TRUE)
options(knitr.kable.NA = '')

# loading necessary R packages ####
## data manipulation
library(dplyr)    # %>%, data cleaning functions
library(magrittr) # coerce col to factors or numeric

## outputs - graphs, tables
library(ggplot2)  # ggplot(), etc.
library(plotly)  # ggplot(), etc.
library(DT)       # datatable()
library(knitr)    # kable
library(grafify)
library(data.table)
library(shiny)
```

```{r printfxn, include=FALSE}

# functions ####
# for printing tables (data.frames) - DT::datatable()
printTable <- function(DT, pageLength = 7, 
                         numericColNames = NULL, numRound = 3, 
                         scrollXOpt = FALSE, colNames = NULL, autoWidthOpt = FALSE,...) {
    
    DT <- data.frame(lapply(X = DT, 
                            FUN = function(x) {
                              if(is.numeric(x)){
                                round(x, numRound)
                              } else {
                                x
                              }
                            }))
    
    table <- DT::datatable(data = DT, 
                           colnames = colNames,
                           filter = "top", 
                           options = list(autoWidth = autoWidthOpt,
                                          dom = 'l<<t>pB>', 
                                          buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'print'),
                                          pageLength = pageLength,
                                          searchHighlight = TRUE,
                                          lengthMenu = c(7, 14, 21, 28, 35),
                                          scrollX = scrollXOpt),
                           extensions = 'Buttons',
                           rownames = FALSE,
                           ...)
    if (length(numericColNames) > 0){
      table <- table %>% DT::formatRound(columns = numericColNames,
                                     digits = numRound)
    }
    
    table
  }
```
<div style = "width:auto; height:auto; overflow:auto">

```{r plot_init, include = FALSE}
# Init Step to make sure that the dependencies are loaded
# was done to enable proper printing of the tab header and the graphs within the for loop below using print(htmltools::tagList(ggplotly(...)))
library(ggplot2)
htmltools::tagList(plotly::ggplotly(ggplot2::ggplot()))
htmltools::tagList(printTable(mtcars))
# Get the current figure size in pixels:
get_w <- function() {
  with(knitr::opts_current$get(c("fig.width", "dpi", "fig.retina")),
       fig.width*dpi/fig.retina)
}

get_h <- function() {
  with(knitr::opts_current$get(c("fig.height", "dpi", "fig.retina")),
       fig.height*dpi/fig.retina)
}

```
</div>

```{r }

suppressWarnings(tryCatch({
  if(file.exists("./outputs/result.RData")){
    load("./outputs/resultQaPheno.RData")
  }else{
    load("resultQaPheno.RData")
  }
}, error = function(e) {
  shinyjs::hide()
}))

```

### Table of modifications

The following table shows the modifications that have been saved in your analysis object and that will be applied to the raw phenotypic data when this specific time stamp (ID) is selected/used in other analytical modules (e.g., single trial analysis module).

```{r }

if (is.null(result)){
  # invisible(NULL)
} else {
  modifications <- result$modifications$pheno
  idQa <- result$status[which(result$status$module %in% c("qaRaw","qaMb","qaFilter","qaDesign","qaConsist","qaConsistency")),"analysisId"];
  idQa <- idQa[length(idQa)]
  idQaType <- result$status[result$status$analysisId == idQa, "module"]
  modifications <- modifications[which( modifications$analysisId == idQa & modifications$module == idQaType),]
  traitsTable <- unique(modifications[which( modifications$analysisId == idQa),"trait"])
  
  
  if("params" %in% ls()){ # we will download the document
    shinyjs::hide()
  }else{
    inputPanel(
    shiny::selectInput(ns("traitQa"), 
                       label = "Trait to visualize:", 
                       choices = traitsTable, 
    ),
    )
  }
  
}

```

```{r, results='asis'}
if (is.null(result)){
  # invisible(NULL)
} else {
  numericalColumns <- c("value","row") 
  
  if("params" %in% ls()){ # we will download the document
    printTable(modifications, autoWidthOpt = TRUE, scrollXOpt = FALSE,
             colNames = colnames(modifications),
           numericColNames = numericalColumns,
           numRound = 4)
  }else{
    
    DT::renderDT(printTable(modifications[modifications$trait==input$traitQa,],
                        autoWidthOpt = FALSE, scrollXOpt = TRUE,
                        colNames = colnames(modifications[modifications$trait==input$traitQa,]),
                        numericColNames = numericalColumns,
                        numRound = 4), server = FALSE)
  }
}

```

<p>&nbsp;</p>

### Diagnostic plot after modifications
After remove the outlier in te step before, you can see in the plots the final behavior of the residual test.

```{r, results='asis'}
if (is.null(result)){
  # invisible(NULL)
} else {
  resRes<-data.frame(result$predictions)
  idQa <- result$status[which(result$status$module%in%"qaMb"),"analysisId"];
  idQa <- idQa[length(idQa)]
  idQaType <- result$status[result$status$analysisId == idQa, "module"]
  resRes <- resRes[which( resRes$analysisId == idQa & resRes$module == idQaType),]
  traitsResP <- unique(resRes$trait)
  envResP<-unique(resRes$environment)
  if("params" %in% ls()){ # we will download the document
    shinyjs::hide()
  }else{
    inputPanel(
    shiny::selectInput(ns("traitQaResP"), 
                       label = "Trait to visualize:", 
                       choices = traitsResP, 
    ),
    column(width=12),
    shiny::selectInput(ns("envQaResP"), 
                       label = "Environment to vizualize:", 
                       choices = envResP, 
    ),
    )
  }
}
```

<div style = "width:auto; height:900px; overflow:auto">

```{r, results='asis'}
if (is.null(result)){
  # invisible(NULL)
} else {
  if("params" %in% ls()){ # we will download the document
    cat(paste0("###   {.tabset .tabset-pills}    \n\n  "))
    for(iTrait in traitsResP){#iTrait=traitsResP[1]
      #cat("\n\n#### ",iTrait, " {.tabset .tabset-pills}\n\n", sep="")
      for(iEnv in envResP){#iEnv=envResP[1]
        cat("\n\n#### ",iTrait,"_",iEnv," {.tabset .tabset-pills}\n\n", sep="")
        tmpResP <- resRes[resRes$trait == iTrait & resRes$environment == iEnv,]
        tmpResP <- tmpResP[,-c(1,2)]
        tmp <- strsplit(tmpResP[,3], "_")
        tmpResP <- tmpResP[,-3]
        names(tmpResP)<-c("idRow","trait","designation","repF","stdt","outlier",
					"environment","fitted","residuals","cookD","leverage")
        tmpResP$N   <- sapply(tmp, `[`, 1)
        tmpResP$p <- sapply(tmp, `[`, 2)
        colsR <- c("idRow","p","N","fitted","residuals","cookD","leverage","stdt")
        tmpResP[,colsR] <- lapply(tmpResP[,colsR], as.numeric)
        p<-cgiarPipeline::pplotmod(df = tmpResP)
        print(htmltools::tagList(p))
        cat("\n\n")
      }#fin env
      #cat("\n\n")
    }#fin trait
  }else{
    plotly::renderPlotly({
        tmpResP <- resRes[resRes$trait == input$traitQaResP & resRes$environment == input$envQaResP,]
        tmpResP <- tmpResP[,-c(1,2)]
        tmp <- strsplit(tmpResP[,3], "_")
        tmpResP <- tmpResP[,-3]
        names(tmpResP)<-c("idRow","trait","designation","repF","stdt","outlier",
					"environment","fitted","residuals","cookD","leverage")
        tmpResP$N   <- sapply(tmp, `[`, 1)
        tmpResP$p <- sapply(tmp, `[`, 2)
        colsR <- c("idRow","p","N","fitted","residuals","cookD","leverage","stdt")
        tmpResP[,colsR] <- lapply(tmpResP[,colsR], as.numeric)
        p<-cgiarPipeline::pplotmod(df = tmpResP)
        p
    })
  }
}
```
</div>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

### Outlier warnings

The following table shows a summary about a percent of outliers found it.

```{r, results='asis'}
if (is.null(result)){
  # invisible(NULL)
} else {
  mydata <- result$data$pheno
  ### change column names for mapping
  paramsPheno <- result$metadata$pheno
  paramsPheno <- paramsPheno[which(paramsPheno$parameter != "trait"),]
  colnames(mydata) <- cgiarBase::replaceValues(colnames(mydata), 
                                               Search = paramsPheno$value, 
                                               Replace = paramsPheno$parameter )
  ###
  mydata$rowindex <- 1:nrow(mydata)
  mydata[, "environment"] <- as.factor(mydata[, "environment"])
  mydata[, "designation"] <- as.factor(mydata[, "designation"])
  mo <-result$modifications$pheno
  analysisIds <- unique(mo$analysisId)
  analysisIds <- analysisIds[length(analysisIds)]
  mo <- mo[which(mo$analysisId == analysisIds),]
  
 pct_tagged<-list()
for(iTrait in traitsTable){#iTrait=traitsBoxplot[1]
  tmp<-mydata
  tmp$color <- "valid"
  if(nrow(mo) > 0){
    tmp$color[which(tmp$rowindex %in% unique(mo$row[which(mo$trait == iTrait)]))]="tagged"
  }
  tmp$color <- as.factor(tmp$color)
  stmp<-split(tmp,tmp$environment)
  sstmp<-sapply(stmp, function(x){mean(x$color == "tagged", na.rm = TRUE) * 100})
  ssstmp<-data.frame(cbind(environment=names(stmp),pct_outlier=round(sstmp,2)))
  ssstmp$pct_outlier<-as.numeric(ssstmp$pct_outlier)
  ssstmp <- ssstmp %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      quality = case_when(
        pct_outlier < 5  ~ "Good",
        pct_outlier >= 5  &
          pct_outlier < 15 ~ "Moderate",
        pct_outlier >= 15 &
          pct_outlier < 30 ~ "Problematic",
        pct_outlier >= 30 ~ "Potentially unusable"
      )
    )
  ssstmp<-cbind(trait=iTrait,ssstmp)
  pct_tagged[[iTrait]] <-ssstmp 
}    
pct<-do.call(rbind,pct_tagged)
 
  numericalColumns2 <- c("pct_outlier") 
  
  if("params" %in% ls()){ # we will download the document
    printTable(pct, autoWidthOpt = TRUE, scrollXOpt = FALSE,
             colNames = colnames(pct),
           numericColNames = numericalColumns2,
           numRound = 4)|>
    DT::formatStyle('quality',
      backgroundColor = DT::styleEqual(
        c("Good","Moderate","Problematic", "Potentially unusable"),
        c("#A4D65E","#fff3cd","#f8d7da","#FF7F7F")
      ),
      fontWeight = 'bold'
    )
  }else{
    
    DT::renderDT(printTable(pct,
                        autoWidthOpt = FALSE, scrollXOpt = TRUE,
                        colNames = colnames(pct),
                        numericColNames = numericalColumns2,
                        numRound = 4)|>
    DT::formatStyle('quality',
      backgroundColor = DT::styleEqual(
        c("Good","Moderate","Problematic", "Potentially unusable"),
        c("#A4D65E","#fff3cd","#f8d7da","#FF7F7F")
      ),
      fontWeight = 'bold'
    ),server = FALSE)
    
  }
}

```

<p>&nbsp;</p>

### References on methods used

Tukey, J. W. (1977). Exploratory Data Analysis. Section 2C.

McGill, R., Tukey, J. W. and Larsen, W. A. (1978). Variations of box plots. The American Statistician, 32, 12–16. doi:10.2307/2683468.

Velleman, P. F. and Hoaglin, D. C. (1981). Applications, Basics and Computing of Exploratory Data Analysis. Duxbury Press.


<p>&nbsp;</p>
